ligation of left atrial appendage. 01-28


History of Present Illness:
This 66 year old female was admitted to Lakes Casey County Hospital Hospital
on 08-07 with dyspnea and lower extremity edema.  She was
diuresed, ruled out for myocardial infarction and transferred to
Vibra Specialty Hospital for cardiac catherization.  Echo revealed 4 + mitral
regurgitation and she was referred for surgical evaluation. She
was found to be in acute systolic heart failure and was admitted
preoperatively.

TR Gradient (+ RA = PASP):  24 mm Hg  <= 25 mm Hg

Findings
This study was compared to the prior study of 1988-07-03.
LEFT ATRIUM: Moderate LA enlargement.

RIGHT ATRIUM/INTERATRIAL SEPTUM: Normal RA size. A catheter or
pacing wire is seen in the RA.

LEFT VENTRICLE: Mild symmetric LVH. Top normal/borderline
dilated LV cavity size. Moderate-severe global left ventricular
hypokinesis. No resting LVOT gradient.

RIGHT VENTRICLE: Mildly dilated RV cavity. RV function
depressed. Abnormal diastolic septal motion/position consistent
with RV volume overload.

AORTA: Mildly dilated aortic sinus. Mildly dilated ascending
aorta.

AORTIC VALVE: Mildly thickened aortic valve leaflets (3). No AS.
No AR.

MITRAL VALVE: Bioprosthetic mitral valve prosthesis (MVR). MVR
well seated, with normal leaflet/disc motion and transvalvular
gradients. Mild mitral annular calcification.

TRICUSPID VALVE: Normal tricuspid valve leaflets. Tricuspid
valve annuloplasty ring. Tricuspid annuloplasty ring well seated
and is not obstructing flow. Moderate [2+] TR. Normal PA
systolic pressure.

PULMONIC VALVE/PULMONARY ARTERY: Pulmonic valve not visualized.
No PS. Physiologic PR.

PERICARDIUM: No pericardial effusion.

GENERAL COMMENTS: Suboptimal image quality - poor subcostal
views. Suboptimal image quality - poor suprasternal views.
Conclusions
The left atrium is moderately dilated. There is mild symmetric
left ventricular hypertrophy. The left ventricular cavity size
is top normal/borderline dilated. There is moderate to severe
global left ventricular hypokinesis with basal to mid inferior
akinesis (LVEF = 30 %). The right ventricular cavity is mildly
dilated with depressed free wall contractility. There is
abnormal diastolic septal motion/position consistent with right
ventricular volume overload. The aortic root is mildly dilated
at the sinus level. The ascending aorta is mildly dilated. The
aortic valve leaflets (3) are mildly thickened but aortic
stenosis is not present. No aortic regurgitation is seen. A
bioprosthetic mitral valve prosthesis is present. The mitral
prosthesis appears well seated, with normal leaflet/disc motion
and transvalvular gradients. A tricuspid valve annuloplasty ring
is present. The tricuspid annuloplasty ring appears well seated
and is not obstructing flow. Moderate [2+] tricuspid
regurgitation is seen. The estimated pulmonary artery systolic
pressure is normal. There is no pericardial effusion.

Compared with the prior study (images reviewed) of 1988-07-03,
the bioprothestic mitral valve is well seated and well
functioning. The tricuspid ring is well seated and well
functioning. The severity of tricuspid regurgitation has
decreased.
  Electronically signed by Gregory Garza Paul Brewer, MD, Interpreting physician

Approved: MON 2006-05-09 11:34 AM

  Imaging Lab



Brief Hospital Course:
Ms.Mendoza presented to the office for preoperative evaluation
and was admitted to the hospital for acute systolic heart
failure.  Heart failure service was consulted for management and
she was placed on a Lasix drip which was titrated for diuresis,
then zarolyxn added with good results.  The Lasix drip was
decreased and she was aggressively diuresed preoperatively.
Surgery was consulted for a thyroid nodule and recommended
follow up as outpatient for biopsy which  will be arranged after
4 weeks.  Dermatology was consulted in relation to multiple
pruritic nodules with excoriations/ulcerations that began one
month ago and are most likely due to prurigo nodularis with a
distribution of lesions in areas she can reach, which is
consistent with the diagnosis. These were treated with an H1
blocker and Bactroban with improvement and resolution of
itching.  MRSA screen was positive preoperatively and infectious
disease was consulted who  recommended Bactroban nasal
perianally twice a day.  She had a repeat echocardiogram
preoperatively to evaluate tricuspid regurgitation.

On 01-28 she went to the operating room where mitral valve
replacement and tricuspid annuloplasty were performed as noted.
Please refer to DrMueller operative note for further details.
She weaned from bypass on epinephrine, phenylephrine and
propofol. The epinephrine was weaned off over 24 hours and she
was extubated on 02-19, however, required reintubation on the
next day due to respiratory distress. CTs and temporary pacing
wires were removed per protocols.

the phenylephrine was slowly weaned and was shut off on 05-07.
She was extubated again on 12-05 and remained stable. Amiodarone
was utilized for atrial fibrillation with maintenance of sinus
rhythm and beta blockade was reintroduced.
She was transferred to the stepdown floor on 04-19. She
continued to progress and  on POD#11 she was ready for discharge
to rehab for further endurance and increase in activity. All
follow up appointments were advised.

Discharge Instructions:
Please shower daily including washing incisions, no baths or
swimming
Monitor wounds for infection - redness, drainage, or increased
pain
Report any fever greater than 101
Report any weight gain of greater than 2 pounds in 24 hours or 5
pounds in a week
No creams, lotions, powders, or ointments to incisions
No driving for approximately one month
No lifting more than 10 pounds for 10 weeks
Take all medications as ordered

Followup Instructions:
Please call to schedule appointments
Dr Thompson in 4 weeks (634-255-1803)
Dr Sexton after discharge from rehab
thyroid nodule follow up with Dr. Pratt in 4 weeks
